summary: Matched the Mario Cape item unit by adding the missing itmariocape item state table, moving itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed earlier in the source for ordering, updating the relevant sdata2 symbol from a global placeholder to a local @151 label, and flipping melee/it/items/itmariocape.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The decomp bot reported one new match: main/melee/it/items/itmariocape .data from 0.00% to 100.00%, plus +816 linked code bytes and +32 matched data bytes.
what_changed: Added ItemStateTable it_803F70F8[] in src/melee/it/items/itmariocape.c with two entries, both using itMariocape_UnkMotion1_Anim and NULL callbacks.
Moved itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed upward in itmariocape.c, before it_802B2674, without changing its body.
Kept itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed logic as: get Item via GET_ITEM(gobj), and if ip->owner is non-NULL call ftMr_SpecialS_Reset(ip->owner).
Changed config/GALE01/symbols.txt at .sdata2:0x804DCFB8 from global it_804DCFB8 to local @151.
Changed configure.py entry for melee/it/items/itmariocape.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
smart_moves: Resolved the .data match by materializing the item state table rather than leaving the unit dependent on anonymous or missing data.
Used a local @151 sdata2 label for 0x804DCFB8, reflecting that the float constant was local-scope in the matched object rather than a stable global symbol.
Adjusted source declaration order while preserving function behavior, a common matching tactic when the generated object needs symbols/functions emitted in a specific sequence.
Only flipped the configure.py status after the item source and symbol table changes were sufficient to produce a bot-confirmed match.
decomp_lessons: For item files, matching may require restoring ItemStateTable data in the same compilation unit, not only matching code bodies.
Small symbol-scope corrections in config/GALE01/symbols.txt can be decisive for matching data sections.
Moving an unchanged function can affect object layout/order and should be considered when code is logically correct but still nonmatching.
When a unit reaches Matching, update configure.py so future builds track it as matched.
Bot match reports are useful evidence: this PR specifically improved itmariocape .data from 0.00% to 100.00%.
naming_conventions: Item state table was named with address-style convention: it_803F70F8.
Local sdata2 constants can use @NNN labels, as shown by @151 at .sdata2:0x804DCFB8.
Known item callback/function names retain subsystem capitalization, e.g. itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed.
Existing unknown functions remain address-style names such as it_802B2560, it_802B2674, and it_802B2870 when no better semantic name is evidenced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Add missing .data structures to recover data-section matching.
Reorder source-level definitions to match object emission order.
Correct symbol scope from global placeholder to local label for an sdata2 float constant.
Mark the object Matching in configure.py only after code/data layout changes align.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

Automated decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 progress: linked code 30.92% (+0.02%, +816 bytes), matched data 20.98% (+32 bytes), linked data 28.86% (+40 bytes), and one new match for main/melee/it/items/itmariocape .data from 0.00% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs where adding an ItemStateTable caused a .data match.
Search for symbol changes from it_804Dxxxx globals to local @NNN labels in sdata2.
Check nearby Mario item units for similar destroyed callbacks that reset fighter special state.
Look for other nonmatching item files where function reordering alone may affect object layout.
